title: Optimize for robustness with forward race-aware stacking weights, not mean CV alone
summary: Scikit-learn’s guidance is explicit that random CV is unsafe for grouped or time-dependent data.
rationale: Scikit-learn’s guidance is explicit that random CV is unsafe for grouped or time-dependent data. Given the small CV-to-public gap in the current best runs, the next gain may come from reducing optimistic validation and weighting models by worst-fold stability rather than average AUC.
implementation_hint: Evaluate models on forward `Year`/`Race_Year` splits plus leave-one-race-out checks, record both mean and worst-fold AUC, then stack only models that are stable across folds. If calibrating, prefer strictly monotonic sigmoid scaling over isotonic when leaderboard AUC preservation matters.
expected_effect: Lower local-CV optimism and safer public leaderboard performance, even if headline CV moves only slightly.
risk: Can reject aggressive models that look best on mean CV, so local score may stagnate while generalization improves.
